name: NerdSEQ
description: Hardware tracker sequencer. 6 modular CV/trigger tracks plus 2 audio tracks, 175 patterns, 176 patches, 32 tables, 8 automators, 8 envelopes, 12 sample slots. Expander ecosystem for MIDI, triggers, CV16, video.

controls.name: SEQUENCER / PATTERN / PATCH / TABLE / AUTOMATE / PROJECT
controls.description: Six top-row half-size navigation buttons. Each opens its corresponding screen. Hold PATTERN while in Pattern screen to page between patterns with cursor keys.
controls.detail: 6 half-size buttons · context menu via NERD

controls.name: MARK / COPY / DELETE / RECORD / NERD / SETUP
controls.description: Second-row edit functions (via SHIFT). MARK starts selection, COPY/DELETE act on clipboard, RECORD toggles live recording, NERD opens context menu, SETUP global settings.

controls.name: START / STOP
controls.description: Starts/queues patterns. SHIFT+STOP stops sequencer (security: press STOP twice). SHIFT+STOP while stopped resets all gates and issues a reset pulse.

controls.name: SHIFT / OK / DOWN
controls.description: SHIFT accesses secondary button roles and enables fine/coarse value editing. OK confirms/creates patterns and patches. DOWN pages 16 rows (with SHIFT).

controls.name: Cursor keys (4)
controls.description: Navigate rows/columns on all screens. Combined with SHIFT to edit values in place.

controls.name: OFFSET switch (CV)
controls.description: Toggle switch that flips the CV output range between -5V/+5V and 0V/+10V for all 12 CV outputs.

controls.name: OFFSET switch (MOD)
controls.description: Toggle switch for MOD output range, independent of CV offset.

inputs.name: INPUT 1–4 (CV)
inputs.description: Four free-assignable CV inputs for live manipulation, CV recording, or parameter routing.

inputs.name: CLOCK IN
inputs.description: External clock input. Accepts 1/16 ticks, 24 PPQN, or DIN-sync depending on setup.

inputs.name: RESET IN
inputs.description: Reset pulse input. Resets all tracks to first row.

outputs.name: CV 1–6
outputs.description: Six CV outputs — one per modular track. Supports 1V/Oct, 1.2V/Oct, and Volt/Hz ranges per track.

outputs.name: TRIGGER 1–6
outputs.description: Six trigger/gate outputs — one per modular track. Gate length configurable per step.

outputs.name: MOD 1–6
outputs.description: Six modulation outputs — routable to envelopes, automators, or pattern data.

outputs.name: SAMPLE OUT 1 / 2
outputs.description: Two audio outputs from the sample/synth tracks. AC-coupled.

outputs.name: CLOCK OUT
outputs.description: Regular modular clock output. Can emit reset pulse on stop.

outputs.name: RESET OUT
outputs.description: Reset pulse output. Fires on sequencer start or SHIFT+STOP.

behaviors.name: Tracker row-based editing
behaviors.description: Patterns are vertical lists of rows, each row a step. Tracks are columns with note, patch, trigger, FX sub-columns. Workflow inspired by Fasttracker 2 / OctaMED — type notes as 'C-3', 'F#5' rows, not piano-roll. Up to 64 steps per pattern, 175 patterns available.

behaviors.name: Independent track playback
behaviors.trigger: assign different pattern lengths/dividers per track
behaviors.description: All 8 tracks run independently — mix 16-step and 13-step patterns, divide clock per track (÷32 to ×8), or let tracks free-run. Muted tracks still advance (maintain phase) so unmuting returns them in sync; Break commands still fire.

behaviors.name: Patches as macros
behaviors.trigger: PATCH button · assign patch number per step
behaviors.description: 176 patches store complete parameter snapshots (velocity, attack, envelope, FX settings) recallable per step. Patches can be cloned and edited live. Effectively step-level macros for complex-per-note behavior.

behaviors.name: Tables (sequencer within)
behaviors.trigger: TABLE button · call table from pattern
behaviors.description: 32 tables, each a 16-row micro-sequencer that triggers per step. Tables transpose, scale, randomize, or FX the parent track — synced or free-running from main clock. Great for arpeggios, microrhythms, or per-note modulation cycles.

behaviors.name: Automators as LFOs/randoms
behaviors.trigger: AUTOMATE button · assign source and destination
behaviors.description: 8 automator slots generate LFOs, randomness, noise, or envelopes routed via modulation matrix to any internal parameter or external CV/MOD output. Automators operate without consuming track resources.

behaviors.name: 8 ADSR envelopes
behaviors.description: Eight full ADSR envelopes with rich trigger/gate source options — sequencer gates, CV inputs, tables, other automators. Route to CV, MOD, sample volume, or FM parameters.

behaviors.name: Audio tracks as synths
behaviors.description: Two sample outputs can run samples, waveform oscillators, or 4-operator FM synthesis — each audio track has 2 voices (2×2 for 4-voice total). Pitch set per step in note scale; FX include bitcrush, distortion, delay, wavefolder.

behaviors.name: Playback modes
behaviors.description: Forward, backward, pingpong, random, snake, spiral, even, odd, and more — each track independently configurable. Combined with pattern chaining, creates non-linear sequencing without live intervention.

behaviors.name: Probability + ratcheting
behaviors.description: 0–100% probability (1% resolution) assignable to any sequencer row or dedicated functions (CV, Mod, Trigger/Gate, Tables, Samples, FX). Advanced ratcheting settings per step. Per-step groove adds step-length micro-variation for polyrhythms.

behaviors.name: Live pattern launching
behaviors.description: Patterns can be triggered, muted, and soloed independently from sequencer. SHIFT+DOWN+START mutes; SHIFT+UP+START solos. Live editing while playing — pattern creation, recording, and nearly all functions stay accessible during playback.

behaviors.name: CV input recording
behaviors.trigger: RECORD + patch CV source to INPUT jack
behaviors.description: Free-routing of CV inputs to record into CV sub-columns or trigger parameters. Enables CV capture from external sources (keyboards, LFOs) directly onto steps without menu diving.

behaviors.name: Expander ecosystem
behaviors.description: Ribbon connector adds MIDI I/O + Sega gamepad (IO-Expander), 16 trigger outs (Trigger16), 16 CV outs (CV16), HDMI/composite video of the screen (Video-IO), or cartridge-based synth engines (NSA). Up to 64 total outputs with expanders.

behaviors.name: OFFSET range switch
behaviors.description: Two panel toggles change CV and MOD output ranges between bipolar (-5V/+5V) and unipolar (0V/+10V) globally. Useful to match Volt/Hz gear or unipolar modulation destinations without per-track config.

behaviors.name: MIDI polyphonic sequencing
behaviors.description: Per-track: 4 polyphonic notes, Velocity, CC, Pitch bend, Program Change, Channel/Poly Aftertouch, NRPN. Sequences MIDI gear and records MIDI in; recording accepts poly input.

behaviors.name: Launchpad integration
behaviors.description: Launchpad maps pattern rows to grid for finger-drum style entry and step edit. Launchpad Mini/S/Pro/X families supported — mute/solo and step-input layers switch via side buttons.

patchIdeas.name: Classic tracker voice
patchIdeas.patch: track 1 CV 1V/Oct to VCO · track 1 TRIGGER to envelope · envelope to VCA · 16-step pattern with melodic notes · patches add per-step velocity via MOD 1 to VCA CV
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Track 1: CV/Gate, 1V/Oct
* Pattern: 16 steps

VCO:
VCA:
Envelope:

- NerdSEQ (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (MOD 1) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythm per track
patchIdeas.patch: tracks 1-4 pattern lengths 16/13/7/11 · track dividers ÷2, ÷3, ÷4 mixed · shared clock · each TRIGGER feeds a drum · evolving polyrhythmic pattern from same pattern numbers
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Track 1: 16 steps, /2
* Track 2: 13 steps, /3
* Track 3: 7 steps, /4
* Track 4: 11 steps, /1

Drum 1:
Drum 2:
Drum 3:
Drum 4:

- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 3) t> Drum 3 (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 4) t> Drum 4 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Table-driven arpeggio
patchIdeas.patch: pattern plays held C note every bar · table 01 transposes: 0, +4, +7, +12 synced to track clock · creates arpeggio from single pattern note · modulate table choice via automator for evolving chords
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Track 1: CV/Gate
* Table 01: 0 · +4 · +7 · +12
* Pattern row 1: C-3 + T01

VCO:

- NerdSEQ (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: CV-recorded performance
patchIdeas.patch: external keyboard CV into INPUT 1 · track 1 set to record INPUT 1 to CV 1 · RECORD armed · play keyboard live · sequence captures performance with quantization options
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Track 1: record from INPUT 1

Keyboard:

- Keyboard (CV) p> NerdSEQ (INPUT 1)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> NerdSEQ (INPUT 2)
- NerdSEQ (CV 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Probability-driven hats
patchIdeas.patch: track 4 16-step pattern · every even step 100% · every odd 30% probability · table 02 ratchets chosen steps 2-4x at random · generates organic hat variation
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Track 4: 16 steps
* Even steps: 100% prob
* Odd steps: 30% prob · Table 02 ratchet

Hats:

- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 4) t> Hats (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (MOD 4) >> Hats (Accent)

patchIdeas.name: Audio track FM synth
patchIdeas.patch: audio track A set to 4-op FM · sequence notes per step · automator 1 LFO on operator ratio · automator 2 envelope to modulation index · mix with modular CV tracks via SAMPLE OUT 1
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* Audio Track A: 4-op FM
* Automator 1: LFO → op ratio
* Automator 2: ADSR → mod index

- NerdSEQ (SAMPLE OUT 1) -> Output (L)
- NerdSEQ (SAMPLE OUT 2) -> Output (R)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> External Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Pattern launch set
patchIdeas.patch: 8 tracks · 8 different patterns queued via OK · live mute/solo per track using SHIFT+DOWN+START · SHIFT+UP+START solo · build arrangement by pattern chaining · Break command stops individual tracks
patchIdeas.diagram:
NerdSEQ:
* All tracks: queued patterns

- NerdSEQ (CV 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- NerdSEQ (CV 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 1) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (TRIGGER 2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)
- NerdSEQ (SAMPLE OUT 1) -> Mixer (In 1)
- NerdSEQ (CLOCK OUT) c> External (Clock)
